name: Stuffing Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the turkey giblets, butter, and celery tops until done.
2. Toast the bread slices, then break them into small pieces.
3. Add the Pepperidge Farm stuffing to the toast.
4. Cook the chopped onions in stock until tender.
5. When the onions are tender, add them to the bread mix.
6. If the dressing is too dry, add chicken stock for additional moisture.
7. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Form into serving-size balls and place in a buttered baking dish.
9. Spoon additional broth over the balls.
10. Bake for 1 hour or until brown.
This dressing is moist and easy to serve. If you have too much for Thanksgiving, freeze uncooked balls and use them at Christmas, which helps speed up dinner on that busy day. I hope your kids will like this recipe; mine always did. The secret to my dressing is Salt Rising bread. My grandmother always used it with other breads (we had to keep it a secret because my father hated salt rising). It's hard to make just a little dressing, but it always looks like you're fixing for an army.
